findings.description: Header layout changed from two columns to one.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the header contains a logo on the left and a text block (names and titles) on the right. The HTML places the text block below the logo, collapsing the layout into a single left-aligned column.

findings.description: Indentation lost in multi-line subject field.
findings.evidence: The PDF aligns the second and third lines of the 'Subject:' field with the start of the text on the first line. The HTML left-aligns these continuation lines, losing the visual grouping.

findings.description: Decorative footer bar rendered inline within content.
findings.evidence: A solid dark bar, which appears to be a decorative element from the bottom of PDF page 1, is rendered as an inline block element in the middle of the text content on HTML page 2.

findings.description: Loss of bold and underline formatting in list items.
findings.evidence: The keywords at the beginning of each bullet point in Section II (e.g., 'Dam Safety', 'Excavation') are bolded and underlined in the PDF, but are rendered as plain text in the HTML.

findings.description: Loss of bold and underline formatting in list items.
findings.evidence: The keywords at the beginning of each bullet point in Section III (e.g., 'Dam Safety', 'Ash recycling and reuse') are bolded and underlined in the PDF, but are rendered as plain text in the HTML.

findings.description: Loss of bold and underline formatting in list items.
findings.evidence: The keywords at the beginning of the bullet points at the top of the page (e.g., 'Private Well Issues', 'Risk Classification') are bolded and underlined in the PDF, but are rendered as plain text in the HTML.
